title: senml: duplicate map keys silently accepted during decode
description:
File: rust/lichen-senml/src/cbor.rs
Lines: 450-497 (decode loop)

When decoding a CBOR map, if the same key appears multiple times (e.g., two 'name' fields), the decoder silently overwrites the earlier value with the later one. RFC 8949 Section 5.6 says applications SHOULD detect duplicate keys and MAY reject or ignore them.

For SenML, duplicate keys indicate malformed input and could:
1. Mask data corruption or transmission errors
2. Allow an attacker to inject values that override earlier legitimate values
3. Cause different behavior between implementations

Example malicious payload: A record with two 'value' fields - a conformant parser might use the first, this parser uses the last, leading to different sensor readings.

Impact: Silent data corruption; potential security issue with attacker-crafted payloads; interop differences.

Fix: Track which keys have been seen and return InvalidInput on duplicates:
```rust
let mut seen_keys = 0u16;  // bitmap for keys -3 to 6
// In the match:
if seen_keys & (1 << (key + 3)) != 0 {
    return Err(CborError::InvalidInput);
}
seen_keys |= 1 << (key + 3);
```
